# UC-20.1.14 · Savings Plan Utilization and Hourly Burn

## Description

Savings Plan utilization % and unused commitment hours — operational view for FinOps reviews (related to UC-20.2.9).

## Value

Savings Plan utilization % and unused commitment hours — operational view for FinOps reviews (related to UC-20.2.9).

## Implementation

Schedule daily SP utilization CSV from S3. Alert when utilization <90% for 3+ days. Recommend exchange/modify.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: AWS Cost Explorer export, `aws:savings_plan` sourcetype.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Savings Plans utilization report.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Schedule daily SP utilization CSV from S3. Alert when utilization <90% for 3+ days. Recommend exchange/modify.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cloud_cost sourcetype="aws:savings_plan" earliest=-7d
| stats latest(utilization_pct) as util, latest(unused_commitment_hrs) as unused by savings_plan_arn
| where util < 90 OR unused>100
| table savings_plan_arn, util, unused
```

Understanding this SPL

**Savings Plan Utilization and Hourly Burn** — Savings Plan utilization % and unused commitment hours — operational view for FinOps reviews (related to UC-20.2.9).

Documented **Data sources**: Savings Plans utilization report. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): AWS Cost Explorer export, `aws:savings_plan` sourcetype. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cloud_cost; **sourcetype**: aws:savings_plan. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cloud_cost, sourcetype="aws:savings_plan", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by savings_plan_arn** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where util < 90 OR unused>100` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Savings Plan Utilization and Hourly Burn**): table savings_plan_arn, util, unused


Step 3 — Validate
compare with Cost Explorer; compare with the cloud billing console for the same scope, accounts, and time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Gauge (SP utilization), Table (underutilized plans), Line chart (util trend).

## SPL

```spl
index=cloud_cost sourcetype="aws:savings_plan" earliest=-7d
| stats latest(utilization_pct) as util, latest(unused_commitment_hrs) as unused by savings_plan_arn
| where util < 90 OR unused>100
| table savings_plan_arn, util, unused
```

## Visualization

Gauge (SP utilization), Table (underutilized plans), Line chart (util trend).
